questions: reforestation ar afforestation er difference ki?
answers: Reforestation is the process of replanting trees in areas where forests have been cut down or destroyed. It helps restore ecosystems, absorb CO2, and prevent soil erosion.
Reforestation holo ja jayga theke bon kata hoyeche sekhane abar gach lagano. Afforestation holo notun jayga te forest toiri kora jekhane age bon chilona.
Reforestation restores biodiversity, captures carbon dioxide, prevents flooding, improves air quality, and provides habitats for wildlife. Many countries have massive tree-planting initiatives.
Successful reforestation requires planting native species, maintaining biodiversity, and long-term care. Organizations like One Tree Planted and the Billion Tree Campaign are leading global efforts.
